questions.question: What is the purpose of a stem-and-leaf plot in data analysis?

options.A: To compare two sets of data
options.B: To display the distribution of a dataset
options.C: To calculate the mean of a dataset
options.E: To find the median of a dataset

questions.question: A space experiment measured the temperature of a planet in $\degree C$. The results were: $25, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32$. Which stem-and-leaf plot represents this data?
In a stem-and-leaf plot, what does the 'stem' represent?

options.A: The ones digit of a number
options.B: The tens digit of a number
options.C: The hundreds digit of a number
options.D: The thousands digit of a number
options.E: The decimal part of a number

questions.question: A lab experiment measured the pH levels of $12$ different solutions. The results were: $4.2, 4.5, 4.8, 5.1, 5.4, 5.7, 6.0, 6.3, 6.6, 6.9, 7.2, 7.5$. Which of the following is the correct stem-and-leaf plot for this data?
What is the primary advantage of using a stem-and-leaf plot?

options.A: It can display multiple datasets at once
options.B: It can calculate the mean and median of a dataset
options.C: It can display the distribution of a dataset
options.D: It can compare two or more datasets
options.E: It can create a box-and-whisker plot

questions.question: A dataset has the following stem-and-leaf plot: $3 | 1, 4, 7$. What are the actual values in the dataset?
Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a stem-and-leaf plot?

options.A: It displays the distribution of a dataset
options.C: It is used to calculate the mean of a dataset
options.D: It displays the actual values in a dataset
options.E: It can create a box-and-whisker plot

questions.question: What is the difference between a stem-and-leaf plot and a histogram?

options.A: A stem-and-leaf plot displays the actual values, while a histogram does not
options.B: A stem-and-leaf plot is used for categorical data, while a histogram is used for numerical data
options.C: A stem-and-leaf plot is used for small datasets, while a histogram is used for large datasets
options.D: A stem-and-leaf plot displays the distribution of a dataset, while a histogram displays the frequency of a dataset
options.E: A stem-and-leaf plot is used for numerical data, while a histogram is used for categorical data

questions.question: The following dataset represents the masses of $10$ different asteroids in grams: $100, 120, 150, 180, 200, 220, 250, 280, 300, 320$. Create a stem-and-leaf plot for this dataset and describe the distribution of the masses.

A space agency collected data on the orbital periods of $8$ different planets in our solar system. The data is as follows: $10.2, 12.5, 15.8, 18.1, 20.4, 22.7, 25.0, 27.3$. Create a stem-and-leaf plot for this data and interpret the distribution of the orbital periods.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure to check for any missing values in the dataset
Tip 2: Use the correct scale when creating a stem-and-leaf plot
Tip 3: Pay attention to the units of measurement when interpreting the distribution of a dataset
